basics.name: Mattia Asti
basics.label: Software Engineer
basics.summary: I'm a software engineer with over 14 years of experience, currently based in London, UK. I work best with TypeScript across the full stack while specializing in the frontend, where I've been using React for years. These days, a lot of my work is AI-assisted, and I've used Claude Code heavily over the past year. I'm the type of engineer who thrives at the intersection of product and code; I genuinely enjoy talking to users, understanding their problems, and shaping solutions that address real needs. I'm a big fan of home automation and love finding new ways to use my collection of Raspberry Pis.

work.name: Apple
work.summary: I'm part of Apple Services Engineering where I work on internal tooling allowing us to scale our localization across 175 countries and over 50 languages.
highlights: Replaced an opaque AI black box with an agentic editorial workflow users can see and steer at every step.
Built a remote caching service in Rust used by 20+ teams. Saved 6k hours/year in compute and engineering costs.
Cut analytics feature delivery from 3 months to 2 weeks by applying AI across the whole workflow: design and process, not just code.
Cut CI from 30 minutes to ~3 across a 9-app monorepo with state-of-the-art tooling.
Organized high-profile community events with engineering directors, including an EMEA frontend conference and a talk with the creator of Bun.
Authored company-wide shared libraries and best-practice guides; my internal Playwright Docker image has 100k+ downloads.

work.name: tru.ID (IDlayr)
work.summary: IDlayr was a startup transforming authentication with SIM security. I was the technical lead for the Developer Portal which included: public docs, code samples and a console dashboard. I've done extensive work on the OIDC authentication layer.

work.name: 11:FS
work.summary: 11:FS was a leading fintech consultancy. I built their authentication layer with Auth0, including the architecture of a mobile passwordless login. I was one of the engineers responsible for running demos to our existing and potential clients, including top-tier banks.

work.name: TES Global
work.summary: TES was one of the largest professional networks in the world with 8M+ teachers. I worked on a significant refactoring of a payment gateway used in the resources marketplace, the highest-traffic part of the website with ~500k daily views.

work.name: Hextech
work.summary: Started as a side project with friends working in our spare time during evenings. Built two mobile apps and attempted a fintech-education startup for kids. Learned a lot from the failures.

work.name: TechHub
work.summary: TechHub was the leading co-working space in London. I've integrated our backend systems with different payment gateways and dealt with localization after launching 5 international locations. I also built the internal CRM, website, and a mobile app for the members.
